attributes.content:
## Overview

The Image Search card gives the workspace a structured home for image search interface: query, results grid, source attribution, and save-to-collection.

As a first-class Boxel card, it can be linked to adjacent records, surfaced in filtered views, and reused in automation without the information collapsing into disconnected notes or one-off documents.

## Core Mechanics

Image Search acts as the canonical record for this concept in the workspace. Its fields capture the facts that need to stay structured, while computed state and relationships make it easy to search, group, and reuse across the system.

The card can be created directly by a user, produced from a [[Workflow Playbook]], or updated in response to a [[Trigger]]. When AI is involved, a [[Prompt Template]] or [[Skill Card]] can shape how it is drafted, summarized, classified, or routed.

## Integration Points

- **[[Trigger]]** -- Changes to Image Search can fire automation, and automation can also create or update Image Search.
- **[[Task]]** -- Image Search often creates follow-up work or is itself tracked through task execution.
- **[[Workflow Playbook]]** -- Image Search can be created, reviewed, or progressed as one step inside a repeatable workflow.
- **[[Report]]** -- Image Search can feed rollups and summaries that surface trends, status, or outcomes.
- **[[Document]]** -- Image Search can link to longer-form supporting content when a lightweight card needs deeper context.
- **[[Category]]** -- Category can provide adjacent context, downstream output, or grouped navigation around Image Search.

## Example Use Cases

- **System of record:** Teams create Image Search as the structured home for image search interface: query, results grid, source attribution, and save-to-collection and keep related work linked instead of scattered across notes.
- **Automation handoff:** A [[Workflow Playbook]] or [[Trigger]] creates or updates Image Search when upstream conditions are met, reducing repetitive setup work.
- **Search and reporting:** Image Search stays queryable for dashboards, summaries, and downstream actions without re-entering the same information in multiple places.
